element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns aerial positioning before combat intensity escalates
Decoupled aim from movement direction—core mechanic introduced immediately
Orbiting support fire; positioning learned through early encounters
Simple projectile patterns establish threat vocabulary
Economic decision-making; players choose weapon upgrades and consumables
Progressive loadout customization expands tactical options
Late-game intensity requires mastered aiming and positioning
Each god serves as unique pattern/skill check
Culmination of all learned mechanics; ultimate test

typical_relief_triggers: Item shop interludes (pause for purchasing decisions)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Steady escalation from Stages 1–3; moderate tension peaks at boss encounters; significant intensity ramp Stages 4–8; climactic final stage. Item shops provide breathing room and economic decision points that create natural tension/relief cycles.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Zenny scattered from defeated enemies rewards exploration of full screen space; satellite positioning incentivizes using vertical and diagonal areas

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction (Stages 1–2): basic movement and aiming learned; enemy patterns simple; Zenny collection establishes reward loop; first shops introduce upgrade system without punishing early choice mistakes
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate escalation (Stages 3–5): enemy density increases; projectile patterns become more complex; players expected to master decoupled aim-movement; boss difficulty rises; resource management (Zenny allocation) becomes strategic
difficulty_curve.late_game: High intensity (Stages 6–9): dense waves; rapid projectile cascades; boss patterns demand frame-perfect positioning; Megacrush becomes essential panic option; final boss (Bios) synthesizes all eight god patterns learned in previous stages
notable_spikes: Each god boss encounter (pattern learning required)

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard: scrolling enemy encounters → mid-stage shop break → intensifying waves → boss battle. Nine total stages, each with distinct thematic boss (eight gods + final Bios). Shop interludes provide rhythm breaks and tactical preparation windows.
Each of eight evil gods serves dual role: thematic antagonist and mechanical skill gate. Boss patterns teach mastery of decoupled aiming; purchasable tips support progression without hard blocking.

Zenny currency from enemies funds weapon modules and consumables. Item shop choices create strategic decision points; poor early choices can be recovered from, but optimal loadouts emerge through replay and learning.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Integrated into all stages; shops appear mid-stage
